_meta.author: refine-scorecard (research assistant)
_meta.note: Evidence-based finalization of Victor Anderson (GA HD-10, R). Incumbent (took office Jan 2021), re-elected 2024 over Paulette Williams, running in May 19 2026 GOP primary. State tier. Sources: official GA bio, Ballotpedia, Wikipedia, Turning Point Action scorecard (100 lifetime / 94.57 2026), iVoterGuide candidate questionnaire, LegiScan/FastDemocracy roll calls.

set.office: State Representative (HD-10, sitting; 2026 GOP primary candidate)

profile.religion: Southern Baptist; deacon at B.C. Grant Baptist Church
profile.education: B.S. Mechanical Engineering (Honors), Georgia Tech; Habersham Central H.S.
profile.background: Life-long HD-10 resident (Rabun Co. + part of Habersham Co.). Small-business owner: co-owner Anderson Carpet One Floor & Home; partner in Better Body Fitness 24/7 and Mountain View Motorsports. 10 yrs at Georgia Power. Habersham Co. District 3 commissioner (2014, incl. 2 yrs as chair); chaired Habersham Co. Hospital Authority. Elected to GA House 2020.
profile.confidence_note: Refined 2026-06 from voting record + scorecards + candidate questionnaire. Web-verified sitting incumbent and active 2026 GOP-primary candidate (Ballotpedia, Vote-USA).

victor-anderson.notes_append: EVIDENCE FINALIZATION 2026-06: Verified sitting R incumbent HD-10 (assumed office 11 Jan 2021, succeeded Terry Rogers; defeated Paulette Williams Nov 2024). Running May 19 2026 GOP primary. Deacon, B.C. Grant Baptist Church. HARD record: Turning Point Action lifetime key-issues 100, 2026 session 94.57 (American Culture 100, Border 100, Educational Freedom 100, Taxes 100, Election Integrity 83.3, Health/Medical Freedom 83.3). Documented YES votes: SB1-2025 (girls' sports / Fair & Safe Athletic Opportunities), SB36-2025 (Religious Freedom Restoration Act), SB185-2025 (state medical-transition restrictions), HB268-2025 (Safe Classrooms Act), SB233-2024 & 2023 (Georgia Promise Scholarship voucher/ESA), HB963/HB350/HB1001/HB1076 2026, SB291/SB13 2025. iVoterGuide questionnaire (overall 'Conservative, conditional', grade 55): pro-life except imminent danger to mother, supports Born-Alive Act, opposes taxpayer abortion funding; affirms one-man-one-woman marriage, opposes SOGI protected classes; strongly supports Christian religious expression; pro-border-wall, anti-sanctuary; opposes voting without photo ID; supports armed teachers. NO CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION found — consistent down-the-line GA conservative. No GRTL endorsement/rating confirmed in available sources (NO EVIDENCE FOUND). No NRA/GOA letter-grade located. HB481 heartbeat (2019) PREDATES his tenure — never voted on it.

0.note: HARD (questionnaire). iVoterGuide: supports Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act, abortion only when mother's life in imminent danger; 2020 questionnaire 'Human life begins at conception... legal protection at every stage.'

1.note: SOFT/HARD. Restrictionist, not abolitionist: accepts life-of-mother exception; no vote/advocacy for full abolition. Rubric q1 = abolition (not merely restrictions).

2.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on embryonic stem cells / IVF embryo discard / chimeric research.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on euthanasia / assisted suicide position.

4.note: HARD. R rated by pro-life groups; no record of Planned Parenthood/NARAL/EMILY's List PAC money. GRTL endorsement NOT confirmed (sources unavailable).

0.note: HARD (questionnaire). 'Strongly agrees' government should define marriage as only a union of one man and one woman.

1.note: HARD. Same questionnaire stance opposing legal recognition of same-sex marriage.

2.note: HARD (votes). YES on SB1-2025 (girls' sports, biological sex) and SB185-2025 (medical-transition restrictions); GOP near-party-line on SB140-2023 minors gender-care ban (96-75).

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: HARD. Opposes adding SOGI as protected classes; YES on HB268-2025 Safe Classrooms Act.

0.note: HARD (vote). YES on SB233 Georgia Promise Scholarship (voucher/ESA) both 2023 (85-89) and 2024 final passage (91-82). TPAction Educational Freedom 100%.

1.note: HARD. YES on SB185-2025 (parental/medical-transition restrictions); aligns with parental-consent on gender care. iVoterGuide pro-parental-rights.

2.note: HARD (vote). YES on HB268-2025 Safe Classrooms Act; opposes SOGI/gender-ideology curricula.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a specific vote on porn age-verification (HB3). No documented position located.

4.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on faith-based adoption agencies specifically.

0.note: HARD (questionnaire). 'Strongly supports protecting Christian religious expression' across spheres.

1.note: HARD (vote). YES on SB36-2025, Georgia Religious Freedom Restoration Act (conscience protection).

2.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a specific vote/statement on compelled-pronoun mandates.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on public prayer / Sabbath / religious-display votes.

4.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on opposing state-funded non-Christian displays.

0.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a specific CBDC vote/statement.

2.note: HARD (vote). YES on HB1001-2026 income-tax reduction; TPAction Taxes & Spending 100%. Fiscal-conservative record; no documented balanced-budget-amendment vote (proxy via tax-cut votes).

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on usury/debt-slavery policy.

4.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a specific anti-ESG/WEF vote.

0.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on hand-counted paper ballots / QR-code elimination position.

1.note: HARD (questionnaire). 'Strongly opposes voting without photo identification.'

2.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on single-day-voting / absentee-restriction position.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a specific mail-in/drop-box/harvesting vote (SB202 predates? assumed office Jan 2021; SB202 passed Mar 2021 — vote not confirmed in sources).

4.note: HARD (vote). YES on HB963-2026 banning foreign-national campaign funding (Zuckerbucks/foreign-money guardrail); TPAction Election Integrity 83.3%.

0.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of an affirmative pro-police program/statement meeting the high bar (routine R alignment insufficient).

1.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a confirmed SB63 (cashless bail) or SB92 (prosecutor oversight) roll-call vote in available sources.

2.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on victim-restitution position.

0.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a 10th-Amendment nullification sponsorship.

2.note: HARD (votes). State-level resistance to federal direction on gender care (SB185-2025) and religious liberty (SB36-2025 RFRA) against federal SOGI mandates.

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of an explicit state-sovereignty statement.

0.note: HARD (questionnaire). 'Strongly favors border wall construction and complete entry/exit control.' TPAction Border Security 100%.

1.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on mandatory deportation incl. those who entered as minors.

2.note: HARD (questionnaire). Supports denying funds to sanctuary jurisdictions; opposes sanctuary policies. (GA HB1105-2024 anti-sanctuary alignment, vote not individually confirmed.)

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a specific E-Verify vote/statement.

4.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on birthright citizenship.

0.note: HARD (questionnaire). Believes 'no restrictions on gun ownership are needed for public safety'; supports armed teachers. (SB319 constitutional carry passed Apr 2022 during his tenure; individual roll call not confirmed in sources.)

1.note: HARD (questionnaire). No-restrictions stance implies opposition to red-flag / magazine / 'assault-weapon' limits.

2.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND on NFA/GCA repeal (federal-level).

3.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a specific castle-doctrine / stand-your-ground vote.

4.note: NO EVIDENCE FOUND. No NRA/GOA letter-grade located.
